rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Six States Distributors, Inc.¿s amended motion to quash service of summons is continued to October 5, 2011 per agreement of the parties. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jaguar Land Rover Of North America, Llc'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011, line 1. Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC¿s demurrer will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s special demurrers to the third (false representation), fourth (intentional tort), sixth (negligence ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) and seventh (strict liability ¿ vehicle, clutch and brake components) causes of action of plaintiffs¿ complaint are overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations to plead those causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jaguar Land Rover Of North America, Llc'S Motion To Strike The Punitive Damages Prayer And Allegations And The Sixth And Seventh Causes Of Action From Plaintiffs' Complaint For Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011, line 1. Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion is denied. As to punitive damages, plaintiffs sufficiently pled that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression. As to the sixth and seventh causes of action, defendant¿s motion is denied as moot based on the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer regarding those two causes of action. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Rockwell International Corp.¿s second amended demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 6th causes of action in plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/HEK)
